summary: DTS 5.1 surround encoding gives brilliant audio when played on a proper setup, but many users hear only static; the show is solid with a highlighted “St. Stephen.”
review: The recording is a DTS‑encoded matrix mix that sounds spectacular in a 5.1 system, though listeners without DTS hardware report silence or static. Musically the concert is deemed good – not legendary – with “St. Stephen” singled out as a strong performance. Overall opinions are split between technical frustration and high‑fidelity appreciation.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is modestly positive (good but not great) while recording quality ranges from poor (static on standard players) to excellent (surround‑sound experience), yielding a mid‑high rating with mixed confidence due to divergent user experiences.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Encoded as DTS 5.1; plays cleanly only on DTS‑capable receivers, otherwise produces static or silence for most players. Reviewers praise the surround mix (SBD front, AUD rear) when properly decoded.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Overall considered a solid performance – good rather than great – with no specific criticisms of pacing or transitions.
